title: zzop NormalizedEnvelope v1
description: External-parser / adapter-overlay envelope contract (docs/NORMALIZED_AST.md; crates/core/src/normalized.rs). Field names, required-ness, and nullability below mirror the Rust serde types field-for-field — a field is 'required' here exactly when its Rust struct field has no #[serde(default)], and 'nullable' exactly when its Rust type is Option<T> deserialized without #[serde(default)] (present key, value may be null) as opposed to omittable-and-defaulting.
$comment: TOLERANCE CONTRACT: additionalProperties is true at every object level in this schema except the three externally-tagged enum wrapper objects (procedureRouterEntry, routerMountEntry, entityRef), whose single-discriminator shape is closed. The engine silently ignores fields it does not recognize — it never hard-fails on an unknown field, AND never warns about one either (serde drops unrecognized fields by default; no unknown-field warning code exists anywhere in this crate's envelope/overlay ingestion path). Same tolerant-input philosophy as zzop's config surface (see docs/adapters/README.md's 'Envelope schema & versioning policy'), except that the config surface additionally WARNS on an unknown key via an explicit vocabulary walk (crates/config/src/mapper.rs, warn_unknown_keys) — the envelope path has no such warning layer. A producer emitting a field ahead of this schema, or omitting any field marked optional below, still produces a valid v1 envelope. Only a BREAKING change (removing, renaming, or re-typing a REQUIRED field) needs a new envelope `version`; additive fields never do.

format.description: Must be exactly this string — zzop_core::validate_envelope rejects any other value.

version.description: Contract version. This schema documents v1; a consumer rejects version > SUPPORTED_NORMALIZED_AST_VERSION.

parser.description: "<parser id>/<impl version>" — doubles as the cache fingerprint segment; bump the impl version whenever the projection changes for identical input.

source.description: Tree/source id — the cross-layer join's per-tree tag.

const_map_fragment.description: identifier -> literal string value, for this file's top-level const string bindings.

class_shape_fragments.description: Class field-shape fragments (body-shape-v1) — the DTO-resolution substrate for IoProvide.body.dtoRef (crates/core/src/fragments.rs ClassShapeFragment). Absent = this producer doesn't extract class shapes; still a fully valid, non-degraded projection.

attributes.description: Generic entity-attribute annotations (attributes-v1) — open-vocab cross-cutting facts a producer attaches to entities (a route, symbol, file, or path scope) that per-file extraction can't see, e.g. { target: { pathScope: { prefix: "/admin" } }, key: "auth-guarded", value: true }. Consumed BY KEY by rules (zzop_core::AttributeStore); the contract/kernel is agnostic to `key`. Absent = no annotations.

loop_spans.description: Per-file loop-body line spans (1-based, inclusive) — external-parser counterpart of zzop_core::dsl::SourceFile::loop_spans; each Rust (u32, u32) tuple serializes as a 2-element JSON array. Absent = empty = MethodScan::trigger_in_loop silently skips this file (graceful degrade, same as symbols/io). Canonical wire name is snake_case loop_spans (FileProjection has no rename_all); the frozen camelCase spelling loopSpans is accepted as an input-only alias (#[serde(alias = "loopSpans")]) — never emitted, so a serialized envelope only ever carries loop_spans.

degraded.description: The parser could not fully process this file (size cap, syntax failure); loc must still be present.

is_entry.description: Framework/runtime entry loaded by convention rather than imported (e.g. SvelteKit hooks.*/+page, a .vue route) — exempts the file from dead-candidates/unreachable. Meaningful only in Mode B (adapter overlay) composition; Mode A (analyzeEnvelope) does not read this field.

isDefault.description: export default function — also matchable via the file#default key. This IS the canonical wire name (SourceSymbol is #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")], which governs the OUTPUT name regardless of the alias below). DRIFT NOTE: an earlier revision of this schema documented the snake_case spelling (is_default) as the primary property, which is backwards — alias only affects deserialization, never the serialized key. The frozen-v1 snake_case spelling is_default is still accepted ON INPUT via #[serde(alias = "is_default")], but a conforming producer round-tripping through this engine, or hand-writing a fresh (non-frozen-v1) envelope, will see/should emit isDefault.

bodyStart.description: Body line range start (1-based, inclusive) for functions/classes; absent/null for type/interface or a parser that cannot produce spans (those files silently skip method-scan DSL rules — never an error). Canonical wire name; frozen-v1 snake_case body_start is an accepted input-only alias (#[serde(alias = "body_start")]) — see isDefault's description for why alias never changes the OUTPUT key.

bodyEnd.description: Canonical wire name; frozen-v1 snake_case body_end is an accepted input-only alias (#[serde(alias = "body_end")]).

writeSites.description: Pre-computed store-write sites within this symbol's body span, in source order. Optional producer-side; almost never populated by a non-TS adapter. IMPORTANT: unlike is_default/body_start/body_end this field has NO frozen snake_case alias — writeSites (camelCase) is the ONLY accepted name; a write_sites key is silently dropped by the tolerant reader.

sink.description: Short human-readable label ("prisma.user.update", or the first few tokens of a raw-SQL statement).

kind.description: Set only when the write also qualifies as non-idempotent; kebab-case NonIdempotentKind wire form (crates/core/src/ir.rs, #[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]). null for a plain idempotent update or a raw-SQL write.

specifier.description: Verbatim specifier from the import "..." statement ("@/features/x", "./foo").

original.description: Original exported name: default import = "default", namespace = "*".

deferred.description: A CommonJS require() nested in a function body — a lazy import.

type_only.description: import type ... — erased by TS at compile time.

original.description: Original name in the source. star = "*".

local_alias.description: Name exposed in the current file. export { A as B } = B, star = "*".

ioFacts.$comment: This nested-required-ness is NOT a typo: IoFacts (crates/core/src/io.rs) derives Default for the container as a whole (so FileProjection.io itself is #[serde(default)] and fully omittable, see fileProjection's io property), but neither `provides` nor `consumes` individually carries #[serde(default)] — so if a `io` OBJECT is present in the JSON at all, both keys must be present inside it (each may still be an empty array).

kind.description: The boundary an interface crosses ("http", "db-table", ...). Open-ended — an adapter may introduce its own kind. RESERVED: "nest-global-prefix" is an engine-internal sentinel kind (crates/engine/src/envelope.rs is_reserved_provide_kind) produced only by the native TypeScript parser's NestJS setGlobalPrefix() recognizer and consumed only by the native compose seam that applies + strips it. A producer emitting this kind is out of contract: in envelope ingestion (analyze_envelope) it is dropped at ingestion with no rewrite, with one aggregate warning per envelope naming the producer-forbidden kinds; in adapter-overlay application (apply_adapter_overlays) it is likewise dropped before the merge, with one aggregate warning per overlay naming the producer-forbidden kinds. It never reaches output, findings, or the cross-layer linker.

key.description: Adapter-normalized interface identity, e.g. "GET /users/{}". Must be byte-identical to what the consuming side computes for the exact join to succeed — see lib/keys.js in examples/adapter-kit (embedded: `zzop-mcp contract key-normalization-fixture` pins the exact bytes).

symbol.description: Handler/owner symbol id (e.g. the controller method) for richer edges. Omittable, or explicit null.

body.description: Request-body contract the handler declares, when statically visible (@Body() param with a class DTO type). Additive/optional — absent everywhere it does not apply. Omittable, or explicit null.

ioConsume.$comment: `key` is NOT in `required` even though `IoConsume::key` carries no #[serde(default)]: serde_derive special-cases every `Option<T>`-typed field as implicitly omittable on missing input regardless of an explicit `default` attribute (verified: crates/core/tests/envelope_schema_parity.rs's drop-one-key probe against the real type), so an ABSENT key and an explicit `key: null` deserialize identically to `None` — there is no Rust-enforced distinction between them. This is the general rule for every Option<T> property in this schema: `required` reflects the true (probed) Rust behavior, not just the presence/absence of #[serde(default)].

kind.description: RESERVED: "client-base-prefix" is an engine-internal sentinel kind (crates/engine/src/envelope.rs is_reserved_consume_kind; zzop_parser_typescript::CLIENT_BASE_PREFIX_KIND) produced only by the native TypeScript parser's axios.defaults.baseURL recognizer and consumed only by the native compose seam that applies + strips it. Same out-of-contract posture as ioProvide.kind's "nest-global-prefix": envelope ingestion drops it at ingestion (no rewrite), with one aggregate warning per envelope naming the producer-forbidden kinds; adapter-overlay application drops it before the merge, with one aggregate warning per overlay naming the producer-forbidden kinds. It never reaches output, findings, or the cross-layer linker.

key.description: null OR OMITTED when the adapter could not statically resolve a dynamic target — reported, never force-matched. (Omitted and explicit null are indistinguishable to the engine: Option<String> with no #[serde(default)] is still implicitly omittable, per serde_derive's Option auto-default.)

raw.description: The raw expression text as written at the call site. Set whenever key is null; kept (not cleared) when late cross-file resolution fills key in afterwards, as provenance. Omittable, or explicit null.

method.description: HTTP method of the call site, kept for late re-resolution. Only meaningful when key is null. Omittable, or explicit null.

body.description: Statically witnessed request-body literal shape at the call site, when one is visible. Additive/optional. Omittable, or explicit null.

client.description: Which client recognizer produced this consume ("axios", "ky", "fetch", "$fetch", "angular", or a custom tag set by a generated-SDK injection adapter) — provenance for client-scoped normalization seams (e.g. an axios.defaults.baseURL path prefix must apply to axios call sites only). null = producer doesn't tag (older envelopes, non-egress kinds); a client-scoped seam then leaves the consume untouched. Omittable, or explicit null.

provideBodyField.description: One declared field of a request-body DTO class (crates/core/src/io.rs ProvideBodyField, #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] — a no-op here since both field names are already single words).

optional.description: true when the field is ?-optional or carries an @IsOptional() decorator.

provideBodyShape.description: The request-body contract a route handler declares (@Body() dto: CreateUserDto) — crates/core/src/io.rs ProvideBodyShape, #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]. Emitted by the parser with only dtoRef set; assemble resolves the ref against the tree-wide merged class-shape map and fills fields. An unresolvable or ambiguous ref drops the whole shape (never guessed).

subKey.description: @Body('user') sub-key — the DTO describes body.user, not the body root. null/omitted = root. Omittable, or explicit null (#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]).

dtoRef.description: Unresolved DTO class name as written in the parameter type annotation. Present on parser emit; cleared by assemble once fields is materialized (an adapter overlay may instead supply fields directly and leave this null). Omittable, or explicit null.

fields.description: Resolved DTO fields (empty until assemble resolves dtoRef). #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")] — omittable when empty.

complete.description: false when the DTO's field list may be partial (an extends clause, constructor parameter properties, an index signature, or computed keys) — suppresses "extra key" claims. #[serde(default)] with no skip_serializing_if — always serialized on output, but omittable on input.

consumeBodyShape.description: The statically witnessed shape of a request-body object literal at an HTTP consume site (crates/core/src/io.rs ConsumeBodyShape, #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]). Extraction is evidence-only: keys are recorded exactly as written (dotted paths, depth <= 2), and NOTHING is inferred about parts the literal does not show. A body passed as an identifier/expression is not represented at all (IoConsume.body: null), never approximated.

keys.description: Dotted key paths witnessed in the literal (e.g. "user", "user.email"). A shorthand property ({ user }) contributes its key; its children stay unwitnessed.

completeAt.description: Paths whose DIRECT children are exhaustively listed in keys — "" for the top level. A level containing a spread, computed key, getter, or non-literal nested value is omitted, which suppresses any "missing field" comparison at that level. Wire name is camelCase completeAt (ConsumeBodyShape is #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]); the Rust field is complete_at.

classShapeFragment.description: The field shape of one class declaration — the per-file half of request-body DTO resolution (crates/core/src/fragments.rs ClassShapeFragment; no rename_all, field names are already the wire names). Emitted for EVERY class declaration, field-less ones included. All three fields are REQUIRED (no #[serde(default)] on any of them) even though `fields` is a Vec — a producer that knows no fields for a class must still emit fields: [].

fields.description: Property members with statically known names, in source order.

complete.description: false when the field list may be partial: an extends clause, constructor parameter properties, an index signature, or a computed property key.

name.description: The identifier this router binding is exported as.

procedureRouterEntry.description: Externally-tagged Rust enum (ProcedureRouterEntry, crates/core/src/fragments.rs) — exactly one of Leaf/Ref/Nested present per entry, e.g. { "Leaf": { "key": "getUser", "verb": "QUERY", "line": 12 } }.

specifier.description: Set when ident is imported; null for a same-file binding. Omittable: `Option<String>` with no #[serde(default)] is still implicitly omittable to serde_derive (see ioConsume's `$comment` on this schema's `required` convention for Option<T> fields) — an absent key and an explicit `null` deserialize identically.

name.description: Identifier the router is bound to; "default" for an unbound export default chain.

routerMountEntry.description: Externally-tagged Rust enum (RouterMountEntry, crates/core/src/fragments.rs) — exactly one of Verb/Mount/ScopedAttr present per entry.

path.description: Verbatim string-literal first argument; :param normalized later by the key normalizer, not here.

handler.description: Last argument's name, if identifiable. Omittable — see ioConsume's `$comment` on this schema's `required` convention for Option<T> fields with no #[serde(default)].

attr_keys.description: Attribute keys the producer attaches to this route's composed IoKey (open vocabulary, value implicitly true — e.g. "auth-guarded"). #[serde(default)]: omit when empty.

specifier.description: null = the mounted router is a same-file (local) binding. Omittable — see ioConsume's `$comment` on this schema's `required` convention for Option<T> fields with no #[serde(default)].

attr_keys.description: Producer-judged attribute keys used only when this mount does NOT resolve to a router fragment (an unresolved mount becomes a PathScope attribute per key). #[serde(default)]: omit when empty.

prefix.description: Router-local literal path prefix the attribute covers ("/" = the whole router).

key.description: Open-vocabulary attribute key (producer-owned, e.g. "auth-guarded"); the value is implicitly true.
Producer/rule vocabulary (e.g. "auth-guarded"); the contract is agnostic to it.

value.description: Open JSON value — most consumers use `true` or a scalar. `null`/`false`/`0`/`""` read as absent by presence-style consumers.

entityRef.$comment: Externally-tagged enum (zzop_core::EntityRef): exactly one discriminator key. camelCase.

prefix.description: Path prefix; applies to every http route under it (longest match wins).
